node.title: Tania Niwa - Wooden Māori Culture Puzzles - Set of
node.maindescription: A set of three Māori puzzles. These photographic puzzles feature three iconic images from New Zealand. These area Hongi performed by a school boy and a school girl, four school childrenperforming Kapa haka, and a respected Kaumatua. Each wooden puzzle measures 20 x 30cm and has 24 pieces.These Māori puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are printed directly on to the wooden puzzle. This ensures the puzzles are durable and long lasting, and the printed photographs will not peel off from the puzzle's surface. These wooden Māori puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New ZealandphotographerTania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She isan internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing ss of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

node.title: Tania Niwa - Landscapes of New Zealand Puzzles - Set of 3
node.maindescription: Aset of three photographic puzzles depicting the New Zealand landscape.These wooden New Zealandpuzzles feature three iconic images. These area Marae, a wooden carving and a fern with koru. TheseNew Zealandpuzzles feature beautiful photographs which are printed directly on to the wooden puzzle. This ensures the puzzles are durable and long lasting, and the printed photographs will not peel off from the puzzle's surface. Each wooden puzzle measures 20 x 30cm and has 24 pieces. These wooden New Zealand puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

node.title: Marae Discovery Background
node.maindescription: Explore Māori life with this large background. Featuring a beautiful New Zealand Marae (meeting house), the lightweight background folds flat, and measures 240 x 120cm. Perfect for roleplay and cultural activities. Photograph was taken by Tania Niwa (Grand Master of Photography Australian Institute of Professional Photography). Tania Niwa is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. In the early nineties she started incorporating Māori carvings and symbolism based on the Marae from her own tribewithin her photography and imagery. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Tania's tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of New Zealand's North Isla

node.title: Rocky Beach Discovery Background
node.maindescription: This large background features the stunning scenery of New Zealand and features the natural volcanic rock that is found along the coastline. This scene can be used to inspire role play activities, divide rooms and create beautiful spaces. The Discovery Background scene measures 240w x 120h cm, and can be easily folded flatfor storage.It is made from lightweight, yet durable 5mm thick plastic Corflute.The scene features a full colour photograph. Illustrated accessories are not included. Perfect for role play, the Discovery Background will inspire imaginative and creative play, and help develop language, communication and social skills. Go for a walk on the moon, roam with dinosaurs, or get ready to dive the deep blue sea! This scene can be used as inspiration for discussions on a variety of topics such ashabitats, animals, foot prints, New Zealand, sea life, life cycles, sustainabilityand geography. It can also set the scene for The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

node.title: Rocky Beach Resource Kit with Background
node.maindescription: This beautiful, discovery background features a photograph of a rocky beach scene. The beach scene is printed onto 5mm thick Corflute. The background scene can be folded for easy storage, and is lightweight and easy to carry. Try adding this background to role play to inspire imagination and creativity. Create beautiful play spaces with this background. This set also includes a variety of resources to support play, and all based on the water and beach theme of the discovery background. The contents of this kit may vary, and may vary to what is illustrated. Please see the Kit Components tab on this page to see the full list of included contents. Photograph was taken by New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa. Tania is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing serie of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand. &nbsp

node.title: Nga Pihi 3 - Māori Songboo
node.maindescription: This Māori songbook accompanies the Nga Pihi 3 CD (OD46CD, sold separately). The Māori songbook contains teaching notes, lyrics, translations, chords and melody lines for all songs. Aunty Tawai Te Ranga offers putake for each waiata. It also features large print lyric sheets that can be used in groups. Songs include Inoi mo nga Kaiako, Kia Ora Ra, Waiata Mihimihi, Kai Kei Taku Kete, Manaakitia, Nga Waka E Whitu, Ka Timu te Tai, Me Piki ki te Kopere, Harakeke E, E Toru Nga Mea, Tutira Mai and Ka Oti Nei nga Mahi. Instrumental songs include Waiata Mihimihi - instrumental, Kai Kei Taku Kete - instrumental, Manaakitia - instrumental, Ka Timu te Tai - instrumental, Harakeke E - instrumental, E Toru Nga Mea - instrumental, Tutira Mai - instrumental, Ka Oti Nei nga Mahi - instrumenta

node.title: Nga Pihi 3 - Māori Music C
node.maindescription:
This fresh collection of simple, easy to learn songs in Māori. The songs communicate the Māori way of life, traditional and contemporary. Aunty Tawai Te Rangi's input takes the Nga Pihi series to a new level.
Focusing on simplicity, repetition, variety, and spiritual values, the CD also includes instrumental versions of each song for extra singing and movement exercises. Songbook sold separately (OD46B). Songs include
Inoi mo nga Kaiako, Kia Ora Ra, Waiata Mihimihi, Kai Kei Taku Kete, Manaakitia, Nga Waka E Whitu, Ka Timu te Tai, Me Piki ki te Kopere, Harakeke E, E Toru Nga Mea, Tutira Mai and Ka Oti Nei nga Mahi. Instrumental songs include Waiata Mihimihi - instrumental, Kai Kei Taku Kete - instrumental, Manaakitia - instrumental, Ka Timu te Tai - instrumental, Harakeke E - instrumental, E Toru Nga Mea - instrumental, Tutira Mai - instrumental, Ka Oti Nei nga Mahi - instrumental.

node.title: Nga Pihi 1 - Māori Songboo
node.maindescription: This Māori songbook accompanies the award winning Nga Pihi 1 CD (OD50CD, sold separately). The Māori songbook contains teaching notes, lyrics, translations, chords and melody lines for all songs. It also features large print lyric sheets that can be used in groups. Song Titles include Toru-Wha!, E Kaukau Ika Iti, Awhi Ki Te Whenua, Toku Whanau, Ra Whanau Koa, E Hara I Te Mea, Takatakahia O Wae Wae, Etu Kahikatea and E mo Pep

node.title: Nga Pihi 1 - Māori Music C
node.maindescription: This CD features original songs and well known waiata to delight tamariki. Focusing on simplicity, repetition, variety, and spiritual values, and has won the Best Children's Music Album in the New Zealand Music awards. This Māori music CD also includes instrumental versions of each song for extra singing and movement exercises. Songbook sold separately (OD50B). Song Titles include Toru-Wha!, E Kaukau Ika Iti, Awhi Ki Te Whenua, Toku Whanau, Ra Whanau Koa, E Hara I Te Mea, Takatakahia O Wae Wae, Etu Kahikatea and E mo Pepi. Instrumental Versions include Toru-Wha! - Instrumental, E Kaukau Ika Iti - Instrumental, Awhi Ki Te Whenua - Instrumental, Toku Whanau - Instrumental, Ra Whanau Koa - Instrumental, E Hara I Te Mea - Instrumental, Takatakahia O Wae Wae - Instrumentalk, Etu Kahikatea - Instrumental and. E mo Pepi - Instrumental

node.title: Māori Language Blocks - 24 piece
node.maindescription: This set of 24 wooden embossed blocks feature the Te Reo alphabet. These Māori blocks also include maths symbols, traditional carving patterns, and figures from six iwi throughout New Zealand. The figures of Tne, Tangaroa, Twhiri-mtea, T…matauenga, Haumia-tiketike and Rongo-m-Tne can be stacked on top of one another and are interchanged with tukutuku (ornamental lattice) patterns and letters. These Māori blocks are designed by Wellington artist Johnson Witehira, the blocks are made from basswood. Each block measures 4.5cm. Suitable for children from the age of 2 years. This resource helps children to understand the people and the world around them. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also in an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills. We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au.

node.title: Black Sand Beach Discovery Play Mat
node.maindescription: This beautiful playmat depicts the black sand beaches of New Zealand. The photographic print features a scene depicting black sand, volcanic rock, shells, leaves and paua shells. The play mat measures 150 x 100cm, and is made from a durable polyester, with a non-slip rubber surface on the back of the play mat. Perfect for creative and imaginative role play activities, this playmat will add a beautiful atmosphere to any classroom environment. Use for dividing play spaces or for inspiring discussions on topics such as habitats, animals, coastlines, geography, weather and patterns. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to inorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

node.title: Discovery Playmats - Complete Set of 5
node.maindescription: These beautiful playmats depict different scenes. Ranging from a peaceful creek bed, to dry desert sand, to dry, cracked earth and to a wet, black sandy beach. Ask children to find animal footprints or tracks, leaves, or discuss how the wind and the water affect the earth and the sand. Eachplaymat measures 150 x 100cm, and is made from a durable polyester. They also featurea non-slip rubber surface on the back of the play mat. Perfect for creative and imaginative role play activities, these playmats will add a beautiful atmosphere to any classroom environment. Use for dividing play spaces or for inspiring discussions on topics such as habitats, animals, coastlines, geography, weather and patterns. This beautiful photographic mat is a wonderful base for constructions and block play. Or use them as inspiration or provocations for role play and dramatic play. Theseamazingphotographs have been taken by Australian Geographic and Tania Niwa. Contents may vary. Australian Geographicfeatures the work of many of Australia's top assignment and wildernessphotographers. With attention to the environment and sustainability, Australian Geographic has a genuine focus on promoting the beauty of the native plants and animals of Australia. The Australian Geographic images are a beautiful tribute to the majesty of the world around us. New ZealandphotographerTania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She isan internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. r tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand.

node.title: Kapa Haka Kotiro Girl Dress Up
node.maindescription:
ThisKapa Haka Kotiro girl dress up costume depicts the Kiwi, and is surrounded by the Niho-Taniwha pattern (dragon teeth) and the three Baskets of Knowledge. The costume features a bodice with black underskirt and a short, stylised cotton piupiu (traditional Māori flax skirt) and a tipare (head-band). Features velcro closures. Suitable for children ages 3 - 6 years.
Role play helps children to develop language and communication skills. It develops and promotes creativity and imagination. Dramatic and imaginative play also encourages children to become more confident and develop a stronger sense of identity. When children come together in role play, it aids in their social and emotional development. Listening to others, negotiation and co-operation all result from authentic dramatic play. This multicultural dress up set of costume is excellent for promoting social and imaginative play. Role play and dramatic play encourages language development, teaches daily routines and skills plus develops social interations, sharing and a sense of belonging and identity. Play is an important part of early childhood. As a result play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun.

node.title: Māori Number Cards Set of 3
node.maindescription: This set of language cards includes 30 laminated cards. Each of the Māori number cards measures 10 x 14cm. This set includes 3 different cards for each number (showing the number of objects, showing numerical and written word in Māori and English and showing the Māori language only.) These Māori number cards are designed for native English speakers to learn Te Reo Māori. The Māori number cards are not designed to be used for native speakers of Te Reo Māori to learn to count due to the spelling used in the cards. All of the cards are supplied in a drawstring bag, and the colours and designs may vary. This resource helps children to understand the people and the world around them. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding o community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills.

Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills.

node.title: Striped Teepee and Discovery Playmat Kit
node.maindescription: A wonderful space for play and imagination! This kit includes Striped Teepee (ZH001) and Black Sand Beach Discovery Play Mat (SPP56). The contents of this kit may vary. Children will love playing in this colourful tepee tent. It features a window with cover, front flaps and a floor sheet. Easy to assemble, this teepee will invite exploration and play.The tee pee tent measures 170w x 155cm high. Use with supervision and do not leave outdoors. This beautiful play mat depicts the black sand beaches of New Zealand. The photographic print features a scene depicting black sand, volcanic rock, shells, leaves and paua shells. The play mat measures 150 x 100cm, and is made from a durable polyester, with a non-slip rubber surface on the back of the play mat. Perfect for creative and imaginative role play activities, this play mat will add a beautiful atmosphere to any classroom environment. Use for dividing play spaces or for inspiring discussions on topics such as habitats, animals, coastlines, geography, weather and patterns. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealan

node.title: English-Māori Dictionary Sof
node.maindescription: Pukapuka Tikanga Kupu English-Māori Dictionary by H.M. Ngata is the most comprehensive English-Māori dictionary available, offering over 14,500 headwords. It is the first major dictionary to be written by Māori for the study of te reo Māori. The work was originated more than thirty years ago by Hōri Ngata, and has been compiled by native speakers of the language, including many Ngāti Porou kaumtua, with the help of a team of linguists and editors. The dictionary has a simple format, providing headwords in English and Māori, with sentences illustrating usage. Commentary and grammatical material have been kept to a minimum, a simply set-out guide illustrates how to use the dictionary. Appendices list terms for flora and fauna, points of the compass, major New Zealand towns, the terminology of kinship and categories of possessive relationships. You can also get free access to the electronic Ngata Dictionary in both English-Māori and Māori-English at www.learningmedia.co.nz

node.title: Nest Of Singing Birds
node.maindescription: Nest Of Singing Birds celebrates one hundred years of the New Zealand School Journal. A Nest of Singing Birds celebrates, in words and images, the publication that has shaped in some way New Zealand literature. Over the course of its history The School Journal has attracted works from some of New Zealand's greatest poets, painters and writers, among them Margaret Mahy, James K. Baxter, Janet Frame, Rita Angus, Russell Clark, and Dick Frizzell.Started in 1907, and surviving the educational cuts of the 1950s, the New Zealand School Journal has remained part of New Zealand's educational scene. Produced quarterly by the Schools Publication Branch of the Department of Education, the School Journal was pitched at primary school children, who then went on to read the Post-Primary School Bulletin. The Schools Publication Branch has since become Learning Media, and this restructured organization has now sponsored a centennial publication dedicated to its prized publication. At least some things do not change: whatever the alterations to its style and content, the School Journal remains the School Journal. Rather than change, it has constantly adapted, in recent years to "accommodate more of the young person's world and [End Page 240] world view"skateboarding is depicted but the skaters are wearing helmets and safety gear. Adaptations have also been made for the School Journal to withstand the challenges that television and computer games pose to a culture of reading among children."A Nest of Singing Birds" is how the School Journal's office was described to the poet Alistair Campbell, who himself contributed to, and at one point edited the Journal. A particular strength of the book under review is its lavish illustration with images from the Journals themselves. These commissions for the School Journal meant that the country's artists and wood engravers got much needed work and income. Writers and poets also found an outlet in the School Journal, and in many cases their work for the Journal was the foundation of a career. I know from growing up in New Zealand in the 1950s and 1960s how difficult an environment it was for the arts generally (my parents were musicians), and one wonders what the country's cultural heritage would now be but for impetus provided by the School Journal. Some of this heritage is there to behold in this tastefully-designed, beautifully-presented and amply-proportioned Nest Of Singing Birds book. Gregory O'Brien's lively text traces the development of the School Journal and does not shirk at recounting "conflict and occasional controversy" as the Journal adapted to changing circumstances or when restrained from time to time by societal disapproval. The interplay of personalities and the dynamics of decision-making are particularly well handled, and his discussion of the illustrations is nothing if not well-informed. All the same, and while accepting that O'Brien has avowedly emphasized the contributing artists and writers, certain themes warrant further attention. There is attenuated discussion on how the School Journal may have influenced its readersthat is to say, how it might have imparted a broader vision of New Zealand and the world, and inculcated "acceptable" attitudes toward country and Empire [pp. 148-52]and no references to the secondary literature in the field.1 Second, there is scope for discussion on relating the School Journal's content to existing school syllabi. Crucially, the School Journal was far more concerned with local than overseas content. Contrast this, say, with what the British educational authorities were doing in the 1950s for primary school children in Britain's remaining colonies. School text books in Fiji during the 1950s and 1960s exclusively focused on overseas subjects and experiences.2 There is nothing wrong with gaining, through books, a little of the wisdom that Ulysses attained through knowing many places and other people's manners, but not to the exclusion of one's own country or locality. Whatever one might say about the ethnocentricity of some of the School Journal's content, or the stylized and romantic depiction of the Māori people, it cannot be said that it screened out the local. In this regard it mght have been useful had O'Brien interviewed people for their memories of the School Journal and how it might have influenced them.These caveats apart, A Nest of Singing Birds is a remarkable publication that successfully integrates text and image and which deserves a readership well beyond New...Learning Media believes that literacy is at the heart of learning. Our carefully levelled print and digital texts for guided, shared and independent reading will support and challenge your students. Backed by thorough research and built on current and effective pedagogy, Learning Media's literacy learning resources help meet the learning and teaching needs of every student and teacher. Learning Media has been a leading education resource supplier for schools in Australian and New Zealand for over 20 years. We bring high-quality, innovative literacy resources to the education community - resources centred on evidence-based instruction and designed with the knowledge of how teachers teach and students learn.

node.title: Maui and the Sun (SE)
node.maindescription: Maui and the Sun is a traditional legend from the Māori people of New Zealand. It explains how Maui and his brothers slow the sun's journey across the sky to make the days longer for eating, fishing, and hunting. Features of the Book - The use of paragraphs to break the text. - The use of direct speech. - The personification of the sun. - The possessive apostrophes - â€œMaui'sâ€ and â€œsun'sâ€. - The distinctive narrative pattern of the story line that allows exploration of characters, plot, and setting. - The frequent use of the contractive apostrophe - â€œcan'tâ€, â€œdon'tâ€, and â€œwe'llâ€. - The word endings - â€œedâ€, â€œnessâ€, and â€œlyâ€. - The additional information and map on the inside back cover. -The stylised illustrations. Purpose Maui and the Sun can be used in a guided reading setting to introduce and reinforce the following skills and strategies: - identifying the plot and characters within the story; - recognising the f different word patterns, inclu ing word endings; - writing using a variety of sentence patterns.

node.title: Raranga Outdoor Recycled Rectangular Floor Mat - 180 x 300cm
node.maindescription: This environmentally friendly outdoor mat is made from recycled plastic. The recycled mat measures 300cm x 180cm. The mat is fade resistant and can be easily hosed for cleaning. As it is made from recycled plastic, this floor mat is an environmentally friendly and sustainable choice. This colourful, floor mat features a Raranga weaving border. The woven links represent unity and togetherness. The middle of the environmentally friendly mat symbolises Taniko, a traditional type of Māori weaving. This recycled mat may slip on hard flooring without a non slip mat underneath. We recommend using a non slip mat under these floor mats when used on hard surfaces. Please see our website for more information on the non slip floor mats (PL204 and PL205). We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au. As per all items left outside this item may heat up in direct sunlight or in hot weather. This item is fade resistant but not fade proof. Repeated exposure to direct sunlight may result in some colour fading. We recommend to use this items in shade or filtered light and for best results do not leave in direct sunlight for long periods of time.

node.title: Felt Stars - Pack of 100
node.maindescription: Assorted coloured felt stars, use them to add texture to your creative classroom activities. These stars are perfect These stars are great for children to pin around the room, they can pretend to be under the night sky. Children can make mobiles with the stars by gluing them to cardboard, then attaching with string or ribbon to 2 pop sticks glued in a "X" shape. The stars are perfect for children to use during Matariki. Children can create lovely star constellations to celebrate the Māori New Year which is held late May early June. Children can paint wonderful backgrounds then glue the stars on top. This gives the piece a lovely three dimensional texture to the art work. The stars can be hung in 3D dioramas or used in puppet shows. Size: 2 to 6cm

node.title: Star Beads 150g
node.maindescription: Wonderfully presented beads for children to thread with. Kids can create lovely necklaces or glue the stars to their craft projects. They area easy to bead, and come in nice colours, purple, white, red, pink and navy. Children enjoy beading, it allows them to use the fine motor skills, and be creative. They can make something for that special someone or for themselves. Either way the children willimmersethemselves into beading. With their concentration and creativity, this is a fantastic craft. Beading brings a good sense of achievement. By creating something that is to be worn allows to express themselves via beading then the satisfaction of seeing the person wearing the child's hand made piece brings the child a lot of pride. Children can use the star beads to use for the whole necklace, or they can use a selected few as a statement piece. Perfect for Matariki in New Zealand which is the celebration of Māori new year held in late May early June. Depending on the stars alignment. The star beads can alo be glued onto craft such as cards, self portrait paintings. They can be hung from 3Ddioramasas stars in the sky. There are many options for these star beads. Children will enjoy working with them. Pack of 150g. Size1.5cm
node.consumerdescription: Wonderfully presented beads for children to thread with. Kids can create lovely necklaces or glue the stars to their craft projects. They area easy to bead, and come in nice colours, purple, white, red, pink and navy. Children enjoy beading, it allows them to use the fine motor skills, and be creative. They can make something for that special someone or for themselves. Either way the children willimmersethemselves into beading. With their concentration and creativity, this is a fantastic craft. Beading brings a good sense of achievement. By creating something that is to be worn allows to express themselves via beading then the satisfaction of seeing the person wearing the child's hand made piece brings the child a lot of pride. Children can use the star beads to use for the whole necklace, or they can use a selected few as a statement piece. Perfect for Matariki in New Zealand which is the celebration of Māori new year held in late May early June. Depending on the stars alignment. The star beads can alo be glued onto craft such as cards, self portrait paintings. They can be hung from 3Ddioramasas stars in the sky. There are many options for these star beads. node.title: Multicoloured Star Rhinestones - Pack of 50
node.maindescription: Create wonderful night skies or show someone they are your star with these fantastic star rhinestones. They are wonderfully presented can bright. The star rhinestones can be added to cards, hair accessories, pictures, collages, 3D dioramas. There are many uses for the star rhinestones. Children can glue the stars to cards to make the card really stand out. These star rhinestones can be used for Matariki - Māori New Year where celebrations are held for a wonderful cluster of stars which appear late May or Early June. Children can celebrate by making pictures using the star rhinestones to represent the formation. Children can create a picture then tell a story that represents the picture. It might of been when the child went camping and saw lots of stars. They might talk about the milky way, or what it might be like to be up there with the stars. A great topic to talk about about is how stars are made or if anyone has seen a shooting star. This conversation maybe easier for children the country regions, bt fascinating for children who is live in urban areas where stars can be harder to see. These stars are fantastic to have in the classroom. They are easy to use and children will love the result they give.   Assorted colours. Pack of 50.

node.title: Make a Poi - Craft Activity
node.maindescription:
Make a Poi - Craft Activity

Get the children learning about New Zealand culture by making this poi they put together and then play with. These will help the children learn about Māori culture in a practical and fun way and the children can use what they have created in their own poi performance. The children will be using skills such ascutting, gluing, sewing and tying. These will bring the children together as they work as a class to make their poi's that they can use in a performance and take home to share with their families. These combine a number of different steps to help the children developtheir craft skills to produce a poi they will be proud of and can use over and over. Makes Approx. 30 pois.

node.title: Weaving Pattern Floor Mat & Non-Slip Mat
node.maindescription: Environmentally friendly Māori floor mat is suitable for outdoors and is made from recycled plastic. The recycled mat measures 180cm x 180cm. The mat is fade resistant and can be easily hosed for cleaning. As it is made from recycled plastic, this floor mat is an environmentally friendly and sustainable choice. This colourful, Māori floor mat features a curl motif, which represents the young leaf of a fern. Around the border, the pattern represents Taniko, which is a traditional weaving technique. The kit also includes a rubber non-slip floor mat. This allows for the recycled floor mat to be used on hard surfaces such as wooden floorboards and concrete flooring without the map slipping. The contents of this kit may vary. For more information see the kit components tab on the website. Through play, children are able to understand the people and the world around them. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. Tse resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills. We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au. As per all items left outside this item may heat up in direct sunlight or in hot weather. This item is fade resistant but not fade proof. Repeated exposure to direct sunlight may result in some colour fading. We recommend to use this items in shade or filtered light and for best results do not leave in direct sunlight for long periods of time. The non-slip is not suitable for outdoor use.

node.title: Raranga Recycled Floor Mat & Non-Slip Mat
node.maindescription: Environmentally friendly, Māori floor mat is made from recycled plastic. The recycled mat measures 300cm x 180cm. The Māori floor mat can be used outdoors, is fade resistant and can be easily hosed for cleaning. As it is made from recycled plastic, this Māori floor mat is an environmentally friendly and sustainable choice. This colourful, Māori floor mat features a Raranga weaving border. The woven links represent unity and togetherness. The middle of the environmentally friendly Māori floor mat symbolises Taniko, a traditional type of Māori weaving. The kit also includes a rubber non-slip floor mat. This allows for the recycled floor mat to be used on hard surfaces such as wooden floorboards and concrete flooring without the map slipping. node.title: Discovery Backgrounds Complete Set - Set of 7
node.maindescription: These Discovery Backgrounds create a wonderful backdrop and scenery for any play space. The kit includes 7 play backgrounds; Beach (INK001), Billabong (INK002) Desert (INK003), Busy City (INK005), Under the Jetty (INK006) Rocky Beach (INK009), and Space (INK010). All of the play backgrounds are sold separately. Contents may vary. Includes photography from Australian Geographic photographers as well as the New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa. Australian Geographic features the work of many of Australia's top assignment and wilderness photographers and illustrators. With attention to the environment and sustainability, Australian Geographic has a genuine focus on promoting the beauty of the native plants and animals of Australia. The Australian Geographic images are a beautiful tribute to the majesty of the world around us. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealan

node.title: Discovery Playmat - Set of 5
node.maindescription: This set of play mats are excellent for any classroom environment. They feature photographs from Australian Geographic's best photographers, as well as New Zealand Master of Photography Tania Niwa. These playmats encourage children to learn and explore habitats, animals, sustainability and the environment. The play mats also inspire creative and imaginative play, as well as creating beautiful play spaces. The kit includes Forest (SPP060), Moon (SPP059), Black Sand (SPP56), Red Sand (SPP53), Snake Trail (SPP54) and Dried Mud (SPP55) Discovery Play Mats. Contents of the kit may vary. Each of the mats measure 150cm wide x 100cm deep. Australian Geographic features the work of many of Australia's top assignment and wilderness photographers and illustrators. With attention to the environment and sustainability, Australian Geographic has a genuine focus on promoting the beauty of the native plants and animals of Australia. node.title: Colouring Templates - Māori - Pack of 1
node.maindescription: Pack of 10 assorted Māori designs on 120gsm board that are plain for children to decorate. These are ideal for photocopying and using over and over again. These help the children with their fine motor and drawing skills encouraging them to stay between the lines and use a range of different colours. These designs are fun and allow the children to colour these in using pencils, crayons or markers. These allow the children to all colour in the one design or they can colour in different designs while learning all about the Māori culture and history. These colouring in sheets are a great activity to do when discussing multicultural and Māori studies. These are great to keep the children busy and they can be used to create a colouring in competition with all the children. Size:

These help the children with their fine motor and drawing skills encouraging them to stay between the lines and use a range of different colours. These designs are fun and allow the children to colour these in using pencils, crayons or markers. These allow the children to all colour in the one design or they can colour in different designs while learning all about the Māori culture and history. These colouring in sheets are a great activity to do when discussing multicultural and Māori studies. These are great to keep the children busy and they can be used to create a colouring in competition with all the children. Size:

node.title: Creative Explorer
node.maindescription: Creative Explorer is an interactive CD-Rom about the arts of New Zealand. This CD-Rom contains information in English, Māori and Samoan covering art forms as diverse as break dancing and Shakespeare. The Creative Explorer CD-ROM provides interactive ideas and inspiration helping students explore their own creativity and to become more involved in the arts. Learning Media believes that literacy is at the heart of learning. Our carefully levelled print and digital texts for guided, shared and independent reading will support and challenge your students. Backed by thorough research and built on current and effective pedagogy, Learning Media's literacy learning resources help meet the learning and teaching needs of every student and teacher. Learning Media has been a leading education resource supplier for schools in Australian and New Zealand for over 20 years. We bring high-quality, innovative literacy resources to the education community - resources centred on evidence-based instruction and designed with theknowledge of how teachers teach and students learn.

node.title: Tania Niwa - New Zealand Landscapes Cushions - Set of 3
node.maindescription: This set of 3 soft, velvet feel New Zealand cushions featuring the beautiful imagery of Tania Niwa. These New Zealand cushions feature ferns, volcanic beaches and paua. Each of the cushion features a different photo on each side. These New Zealand cushions include a thickly padded foam insert. The removable covers are machine washable in cold water. Each of the New Zealand cushions measure 50cm wide x 50cm long. The contents of this kit may vary, please check the kit components section for more up to date information.New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within herubject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills. We love creating products that allow Indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an Indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au.

node.maindescription: This soft, velvet feel Beach Cushion features the beautiful imagery of Tania Niwa. This Beach Cushion features volcanic beaches. The cushion features a different photo on each side. These New Zealand cushions include a thickly padded foam insert. The removable covers are machine washable in cold water. Each of the New Zealand cushions measure 50cm wide x 50cm long. The contents of this kit may vary, please check the kit components section for more up to date information. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's younge Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. We love creating products that allow Indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an Indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au.

node.title: Beach Cushion Cover - 50 x 50cm
node.maindescription: This soft, velvet feel Beach Cushion Cover features the beautiful imagery of Tania Niwa. This Beach Cushion Cover features volcanic beaches. The cushion features a different photo on each side. This removable cover is machine washable in cold water and measures 50cm wide x 50cm long. This is a cushion cover only, no insert is included. node.title: Tania Niwa - Pebbles and Paua Table Runner - 40 x 150cm
node.maindescription: This beautiful table runner features a vibrant photograph of volcanic rock and paua shells. This table runner is perfect for creating a warm and natural environment in the classroom or foyer. The table runner is made from polyester and is machine washable in cold water. It measures 40cm wide x 150cm long and features the amazing photograph from Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photogrher of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand. node.title: Discovery Play Mats - Set of 4
node.maindescription: These discovery play mats are excellent for any classroom environment. This kit features four individual play mats. The kit includes Red Sand (SPP53), Forest Floor (SPP60), Creek Bed (SPP52) and Black Sand (SPP56) Discovery Play Mats. These playmats feature beautiful photographs taken from the Australian Geographic photo library and from Tania Niwa. These photographs illustrate gorgeous scenes from nature and will inspire imaginative and creative role play in children. These mats are excellent for creating mini worlds and for sparking discussions on nature, the environment, animals and habitats. Each play mat is made from a durable polyester top layer and a non-slip rubberised backing, and measures 100cm wide x 150cm long. These small play carpets each measure The contents of this kit may vary. For more up to date kit information, please see the kit components tab below. Australian Geographic features the work of many of Australia's top assignment and wilderness photographers and illustrators. With attention to the environment and sustainability, Australian Geographic has a genuine focus on promoting the beauty of the native plants and animals of Australia. The Australian Geographic images are a beautiful tribute to the majesty of the world around us. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the nortisland of New Zealand.

node.title: What's New At MTA
Tania Niwa - New Zealand Landscapes Puzzles
node.maindescription: A set of 8 New Zealand puzzles. These photographic puzzles depict the New Zealand landscape. These wooden New Zealand puzzles 8 iconic images, such as a Marae, wood carvings, beaches with volcanic rock, ferns and paua shells. A true celebration of the beautiful New Zealand landscape. These puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are digitally printed onto a laminated surface. Each puzzle is hand cut, so will vary slightly to another. Each wooden puzzle measures 20cm x 15cm and has 12 pieces. These wooden New Zealand puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal mages for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

node.title: Tania Niwa - New Zealand Cultural Puzzles
node.maindescription: A set of 8 New Zealand puzzles. These photographic puzzles depict the rich culture of New Zealand. These wooden New Zealand puzzles 8 iconic and cultural images, such as weaving, a Hongi performed by a school boy and a school girl, school children performing Kapa haka, and a respected Kaumatua. These puzzles really are a true celebration of the vibrant culture and history of New Zealand. These puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are digitally printed onto a laminated surface. Each puzzle is hand cut, so will vary slightly to another. Each wooden puzzle measures 20cm x 15cm and has 12 pieces. These wooden New Zealand puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand.

node.retailtitle: Tania Niwa - New Zealand Cultural Puzzles
node.retaildescription: A set of 8 New Zealand puzzles. These photographic puzzles depict the rich culture of New Zealand. These wooden New Zealand puzzles 8 iconic and cultural images, such as weaving, a Hongi performed by a school boy and a school girl, school children performing Kapa haka, and a respected Kaumatua. These puzzles really are a true celebration of the vibrant culture and history of New Zealand. These puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are digitally printed onto a laminated surface. Each puzzle is hand cut, so will vary slightly to another. Each wooden puzzle measures 20cm x 15cm and has 12 pieces. These wooden New Zealand puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her Māori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties Māori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand.

node.title: Tania Niwa - Paua Shell Soft Memory Foam Carpet - 150 x 115cm
node.maindescription: This paua shell design soft mat is made from EVA memory foam. It features a close up image of a Paua Shell by Tania Niwa. The mat measures 150cm wide x 115cm long. This soft floor mat will create a beautiful play space in any classroom. The soft rug mat is made from EVA memory foam. It features a non-slip backing and the top fabric is made from a soft and silky polyester velvet. This oval rug is very soft to touch. It will create a comfortable play space for children. Babies and toddlers will have a sensory experience as they crawl across these mats. Children will be comfortable as they sit and play on the EVA foam. Each carpet features a beautiful digital print. node.title: Te reo Māori Whero Fiction Se
node.maindescription:
Eight Whero (Early level 1) Readers adapted into Māori Language.
Carefully selected to meet the needs of the Te Reo Māori Literacy Curriculum, with a focus on foundation skills and concepts such as: days of the week, art, animals, belonging, whanau and colours. 210 x 160mm.
8 Readers Included. Early Level 1. Red. NZ RR Level 3

node.title: Hairy Maclary and Friends: 123 in Māori and Englis
node.maindescription:
Learn to count in Māori and English, with help from the mischievous Hairy Maclary and his rollicking gang of friends in Hairy Maclary and Friends: 123 Māori and English.
A boisterous bilingual board book that introduces littlies to counting with the help of Hairy Maclary and his friends!
A gorgeous board book with pictures of Hairy Maclary, Scarface Claw and other favourite characters created by Lynley Dodd to help children learn to count from 1-10 in Māori and English.
This special little volume is perfect for the young number learner and helps to incorporate te reo Māori into everyday life.
Contains a full 1-10 counting chart.
Lynley Dodd is one of New Zealand's best known and best loved authors of children's books. In a career spanning four decades she has produced more than thirty original picture books (creating both the story and the illustrations) published around the world in multiple languages. In a recent review for The Sapling, New Zealand librarian and reviewer of children's books Simie Son wrote, "Fostering a love of language since 1983, Hairy Maclary is almost compulsory reading for babies and toddlers. Reading the lovely Zachary Quack Minimonster and Wake Up, Bear, I was reminded at how delightful and clever Lynley Dodd's books are. They are full of joy and rhythm, relatable and perfectly pitched for their intended audience."
Board book 158 x 158cm

node.title: My First Words in Māori - P
node.maindescription:
If you'd like to speak the beautiful Māori language with your kids, this is the book to get you started! My First Words in Māori.
Help your tamariki to korero Māori with this brilliant first words book by Stacey Morrison, gorgeously illustrated by Ali Teo and John O'Reilly.
My First Words in Māori equips your whanau with the first words you need to speak te reo at home together.
With lively pictures labelled in Māori and English, each page introduces the concepts and words children use as they first begin to talk, get to know people and explore the world around them.
Designed by Māori language champion and broadcaster Stacey Morrison for parents and tamariki to read together, with plenty of details in the illustrations to point out and name, scenes include- Taku Tinana/My Body, Taku Whanau/My Family, Taku Whare/My House, Wahi Takaro/At the Park, Tatahi/At the Beach, Te Marae / The Marae - and much more!
This is the perfect book to bring the Māori language into your home and have fun with the kids r language journey.
Paperback 215 x 275mm

node.title: My First Words in Māori: 50 Flashcard
node.maindescription:
From the creators of My First Words in Māori comes this boxed set of illustrated flashcards to assist children with their te reo journey.
Help your tamariki to korero Māori with these illustrated flashcards designed to partner with Stacey Morrison's My First Words in Māori.
Flashcards are a great way to have fun with tamariki on their language journey. Designed by Māori language champion Stacey Morrison, with adorable pictures by Ali Teo and John O'Reilly, this pack includes- 50 picture cards, with the Māori word on one side and the English on the other. Te reo phrases to ask and answer while using the flashcards. Stacey's tips and ideas for different activities and games with the cards.
Suitable for everyone, from preschoolers to adults, these flashcards are a great way to encourage the whole whanau to korero Māori together.
Topics covered are: whanau emotions/feelings clothing animals food and drink
The set has 50-doublesided cards (plus a header card) packaged in a card box for easy st

node.title: Maui and Other Māori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearo
node.maindescription:
A beautiful collection of artist Peter Gossage's beloved Māori myths, in one stunning volume, Maui and Other Māori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearoa.
Peter Gossage's memorable retellings of Māori oral myths have captivated the children of New Zealand for generations. Their dramatic and distinctive illustrations with minimal yet evocative language form a powerful combination, and each has earned its place among the beloved classics of our literature.
These are exciting, magical tales of adventure and intrigue. Several feature the remarkable culture hero Maui - the quick-witted and the trickster - whose exploits include slowing the sun in its course across the sky, fishing up the North Island/Te Ika a Maui, discovering the secret of fire and his attempt to trick the goddess of death and become immortal.
Maui and Other Legends contains eight essential legends. In this volume you will find timeless favourites such as How Maui Found his Mother, Battle of the Mountains, Pania of the Reef and many more.
Threasury includes- How Maui Found his Mother How Maui Found his Father and the Magic Jawbone The Fish of Maui How Maui Slowed the Sun How Maui Found the Secret of Fire How Maui Defied the Goddess of Death Battle of the Mountains Pania of the Reef
Hardback 217 x 302mm

node.title: Traditional Māori Legend
node.maindescription:
Traditional Māori legends. Nga Tai Korero means 'the currents of speech', a reference to the Māori tradition of oral storytelling. In this colourful book, Warren Pohatu retells in simple form fourteen Māori stories and myths that have been passed down over centuries - including old favourites like 'Maui and the Fish', 'Paikea and the Whale', 'Tutanekai and Hinemoa', 'Ngatoroirangi', 'Rona and the Moon', and 'Maui and the Sun'. All the stories are accompanied by Pohatu's vivid double-page illustrations.
Paperback 215 x 23

node.title: Matariki The Māori New Yea
Illustrated Māori Myths and Legend
node.maindescription:
Queenie Rikihana Hyland here brings together a collection of her favourite Māori myths and legends - from the creation and the ever-popular mischief-making of Maui, to the great love stories of Hinemoa and Tutanekai, and Turongo and Mahinarangi. The stories are brought to life by the vivid illustrations of Patrick puru. Illustrated Māori Myths and Legends is a stunning addition to any bookshelf, be that of a child or an adult. Experience it as part of a group being read to or read it alone - either way the stories and the illustrations will fascinate, interest and intrigue readers of all ages.
Paperback 247 x 255

node.title: Māori Myth and Legen
node.maindescription:
The Māori have a rich and colourful tradition of myth and legend - many of their most important and popular tales are retold in this classic, bestselling book.
Written with the general reader in mind, the stories range from the creation of the world to the coming of life, death and knowledge. They incorporate the great god Tane, Maui who tamed the sun, the woman on the moon, monsters, fairies, wondrous birds and moving mountains.
Born in Auckland in 1908, A.W. Reed (Alexander Wyclif) was educated at Mt Albert Grammar School. In 1932, he helped to found a book-publishing business, A.H. & A.W. Reed, one of New Zealand's leading publishing houses of the time with an enduring list of local books. Reed wrote more than 200 books himself, with a range of reference works and popular accounts of Māori culture.
Paperback 129 x 197mm
&nbs

node.retaildescription:
The Māori have a rich and colourful tradition of myth and legend - many of their most important and popular tales are retold in this classic, bestselling book.
Written with the general reader in mind, the stories range from the creation of the world to the coming of life, death and knowledge. They incorporate the great god Tane, Maui who tamed the sun, the woman on the moon, monsters, fairies, wondrous birds and moving mountains.
Born in Auckland in 1908, A.W. Reed (Alexander Wyclif) was educated at Mt Albert Grammar School. In 1932, he helped to found a book-publishing business, A.H. & A.W. Reed, one of New Zealand's leading publishing houses of the time with an enduring list of local books. Reed wrote more than 200 books himself, with a range of reference works and popular accounts of Māori culture.
Paperback 129 x 197mm
&nbs

node.title: Raupo Book of Māori Proverb
node.maindescription:
Raupo Book of Māori Proverbs, (or whakatauki), express the wisdom, wit and commonsense of the Māori people. They are of great value and inspiration to writers, story-tellers and anyone speaking in a Māori setting.
Several hundred proverbs are contained in The Raupo Book of Māori Proverbs, categorised under a large number of diverse headings, with translations and explanations in English. This comprehensive and dependable book serves as both a useful reference and an insight into values of the Māori.
Born in Auckland in 1908, A.W. Reed (Alexander Wyclif) was educated at Mt Albert Grammar School. In 1932, he helped to found a book-publishing business, A.H. & A.W. Reed, one of New Zealand's leading publishing houses of the time with an enduring list of local books. Reed wrote more than 200 books himself, with a range of reference works and popular accounts of Māori culture.
Paperback 130 x

node.title: Waiata Māori Songs in Histor
node.maindescription:
In Waiata our forebears spoke their hearts - in grief and celebration. For many hundreds of years this great oral tradition of song flourished in Aotearoa. During the second half of the nineteenth century, in times of rapid change, Māori scholars recorded for the future the words of thousands of waiata. Their manuscripts were preserved by Pakeha of foresight and commitment, and along with a vast body of other Māori writing they are now accessible in libraries throughout the country. Margaret Orbell has been working with these manuscripts for 25 years. She has come to occupy a special place in Māori scholarship, having brought to light and translated many ancient texts. In this new anthology she places waiata of the nineteenth century in their social and political setting, conveying the poets' responses to their people's trauma. There is a fascinating richness of detail here about traditional Māori life, with insights into the lives of ordinary people as well as into tribal relations and the interaction ofri and Pakeha. Margaret also reveals the great skills of the composers - their use of imagery, rhythm and symbolism, and the profound knowledge they convey. Her authoritative and illuminating commentaries will make this collections hugely interesting to a wide range of readers.
Paperback 156 x 235mm

node.title: Raupo Essential Māori Dictionar
node.maindescription:
The Raupo Essential Māori Dictionary is an invaluable introductory dictionary for students of te reo Māori. It features clear, easy-to-follow Māori-English and English-Māori sections, with the Māori and English alphabets at the top of each page, all the words a learner is likely to encounter, including contemporary usage and modern terms, a section of themed word lists, including days of the week, months of the year, numbers, cities of New Zealand, colours, emotions, actions, parts of the body, in the classroom, and on the marae.
Ross Calman is a freelance writer and editor, and the author of many popular works on Māori language and history - The Treaty of Waitangi, The Raupo Essential Māori Dictionary (with Margaret Sinclair) and The New Zealand Wars among them. He was also the revising editor of A.W. Reed's The Raupo Book of Māori Mythology. Of Ngati Toa, Ngati Raukawa and Ngai Tahu descent, he lives in Wellington with his wife and two children.
Paperback 130

node.title: Taming the Sun: Four Māori Myth
node.maindescription:
Four classic Māori myths beautifully retold within Taming the Sun with stunning illustrations by award winner Gavin Bishop.
Taming the Sun contains four stories, including two well-known legends (Maui And The Sun and Maui And The Big Fish) and two less well-known legends (Rona And The Moon and Kahu The Taniwha).
Aimed at children with reading ages 3-7 years, these myths are simply and yet elegantly written. Gavin's characteristic sense of humour is evident in all of the stories; and while each story is exciting and often a little bit scary, they are resolved beautifully. Gavin allows the essence of the original myths to remain while also re-telling them for a new generation of children.
The illustrations are stunning - the different colour palette for each story and the strong mix of techniques create bold and beautiful images that integrate with the words in a powerful and meaningful way. Taming the Sun is an imaginative, fresh and much-needed addition to the picture book genre, which takes traditional mths to a new level. Created by one of New Zealand's most talented and passionate children's book writers and illustrators.
SHORT-LISTED FOR NZ POST CHILDREN'S BOOK AWARDS 2005.
Gavin Bishop is a highly acclaimed children's book author and illustrator of more than 60 books, whose work ranges from original stories to retellings of Māori myths, European fairy stories, and nursery rhymes.
Born in Invercargill, he spent his childhood in the remote railway settlement of Kingston on the shores of Lake Wakatipu. Studying under Russell Clark and Rudi Gopas, Gavin graduated from the Canterbury University School of Fine Arts with an honours degree in painting. He taught art at Linwood High School and at Christ's College in Christchurch.
Among the numerous fellowships and national book prizes that have been awarded to Gavin throughout his career, highlights are his Te Waka Toi Nga Tohu a Ta Kingi Ihaka/Sir Kingi Ihaka Award in 2018, recognising his lifetime contribution to strengthening Māori art and culture throughis children's books; The Arts Foundation's Mallinson Rendel Illustrators Award in 2013; and the 2000 Storylines Margaret Mahy Medal for lifetime achievement and his distinguished contribution to children's literature in New Zealand. Gavin was made an Officer of the New Zealand Order of Merit in 2013, and President of Honour of the NZ Society of Authors.

node.maindescription: Oral tradition of song and proverbs are celebrated within these two valuable texts from our Māori History of Song and Proverbs Book Pack. The Raupo Book of Māori Proverbs will become a useful class reference and an avenue to develop insight into Māori values. The Waiata Māori Songs in History book represents hundreds of years of great oral tradition and is a classroom must-have resource. node.title: Early Literacy in Te Reo Māor
node.maindescription: Enrich your young students' early literacy with this exceptional Early Literacy in Te Reo Māori book and flashcard pack. The ideally sized bilingual board books introduce counting and colours. My First Words Māori paperback equips students with introductory terms in Māori which is supported by the complementary flashcards pack which is a great way to have fun withÃ‚Â tamariki. PEN3641 Hairy Maclary and Friends: 123 in Māori and English -Ã‚Â Learn to count in Māori and English, with help from the mischievous Hairy Maclary and his rollicking gang of friends in Hairy Maclary and Friends: 123 Māori and English. A boisterous bilingual board book that introduces littlies to counting with the help of Hairy Maclary and his friends! A gorgeous board book with pictures of Hairy Maclary, Scarface Claw and other favourite characters created by Lynley Dodd to help children learn to count from 1-10 in Māori and English. This special little volume is perfect for the young number learner aporate te reo Māori into everyday life. Contains a full 1-10 counting chart. PEN3634 Hairy Maclary and Friends: Colours in Māori and English -Ã‚Â Learn your colours in Māori and English with the help of the mischievous Hairy Maclary and his rollicking gang of friends. A boisterous bilingual board book that introduces littlies to colours with the help of Hairy Maclary and his friends! A gorgeous board book with pictures of Hairy Maclary, Scarface Claw and other favourite characters created by Lynley Dodd to teach children their colours in Māori and English. This special little volume is perfect for the young learner and helps to incorporate te reo Māori into everyday life. Perfect for children ages 3 months to 3 years. PEN3337 My First Words in Māori - PB -Ã‚Â If you'd like to speak the beautiful Māori language with your kids, this is the book to get you started! My First Words in Māori. Help your tamariki to korero Māori with this brilliant first words boon, gorgeously illustrated by Ali Teo and John O'Reilly. My First Words in Māori equips your whanau with the first words you need to speak te reo at home together. With lively pictures labelled in Māori and English, each page introduces the concepts and words children use as they first begin to talk, get to know people and explore the world around them. Designed by Māori language champion and broadcaster Stacey Morrison for parents and tamariki to read together, with plenty of details in the illustrations to point out and name, scenes include- Taku Tinana/My Body, Taku Whanau/My Family, Taku Whare/My House, Wahi Takaro/At the Park, Tatahi/At the Beach, Te Marae / The Marae - and much more! This is the perfect book to bring the Māori language into your home and have fun with the kids on their language journey. Paperback 215 x 275mm PEN4792 My First Words in Māori: 50 Flashcards -Ã‚Â From the creators of My First Words in Māori comes this boxed set of illustrated flashcards to assi with their te reo journey. Help your tamariki to korero Māori with these illustrated flashcards designed to partner with Stacey Morrison's My First Words in Māori. Flashcards are a great way to have fun with tamariki on their language journey. Designed by Māori language champion Stacey Morrison, with adorable pictures by Ali Teo and John O'Reilly, this pack includes:Ã‚Â 50 picture cards, with the Māori word on one side and the English on the other. Te reo phrases to ask and answer while using the flashcards. Stacey's tips and ideas for different activities and games with the cards. Suitable for everyone, from preschoolers to adults, these flashcards are a great way to encourage the whole whanau to korero Māori together. Topics covered are: whanau emotions/feelings clothing animals food and drink The set has 50-doublesided cards (plus a header card) packaged in a card box for easy storage. Content of kit
